Title: Advanced Economies: High versus Low Levels of Technology Diffusion, 2050
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Output gains (percent deviation from steady state) from a 50/50 human-capital/R&D spending mix for different levels of technology diffusion.

Title: Reallocation of Expenditure: Advanced Economies
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Output responses (percent deviation from steady state) to a permanent increase of 1 percent of GDP in the expenditure categories listed in the legend, funded by a cut in public consumption.

Title: Reallocation of Expenditure: Emerging Market and Developing Economies
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Output responses (percent deviation from steady state) to a permanent increase of 1 percent of GDP in the expenditure categories listed in the legend, funded by a cut in public consumption. No R&D channel: the innovation channel is shut down for EMDEs.

Title: Increase in Spending Efficiency: Advanced Economies
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Additional output gains (percent deviation from steady state) from gradually closing gaps in spending efficiency. Gaps are closed by 2050.

Title: Increase in Spending Efficiency: Emerging Market and Developing Economies
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Additional output gains (percent deviation from steady state) from gradually closing gaps in spending efficiency, shown for closing gaps by 2040 and 2050 (horizontal axis) and for different initial efficiency levels (legend).

Title: Advanced Economies: Combining Human Capital and R&D
Subtitle: (Percent deviation from steady state)
Notes: Note: Output gains (percent deviation from steady state) from increasing public investment in human capital, public spending on research and development (R&D), and a 50/50 mix of both, financed by cutting public consumption.

Title: Estimated Spending-Efficiency Gaps by Income Group, 2000–23
Subtitle: (Efficiency gap, one minus the spending efficiency score)
Notes: Note: Appendix figure. Efficiency gap (one minus the spending efficiency score) for infrastructure, health, education, and R&D. Shaded areas show the 25th–75th percentile range across economies within each income group; lines are group medians; circles mark the 2023 value, the calibration reference. R&D is shown for advanced economies only, as the innovation channel is shut down for EMDEs. The paper's human capital gap e^GE combines the health and education categories.
